source_texts.title: Kickoff Interview: AfriStream On-Demand vision, flows, and constraints
source_texts.text:
Amina [Business Owner]: Thanks for making time, everyone. I want us to walk through the experience end-to-end. I keep saying this: I want a site that feels vibrant and modern, very African but welcoming to non-Africans, and it has to be easy for people who are new to buying things online. Nine out of ten first-time internet users should be able to get through without needing to call for help.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: Right, and we also have to be mindful of the older tech in the field. We promised the board we’ll stick to our current hosting and those legacy browsers. Also MSN TV2, remember that one? It’s still in the mix.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: We can do that, but it’s a tight fit. IE 5 and 6, Netscape 6 to 8, Firefox 1.0… plus Windows Media Player 9 for streaming, encoded at 56k and 256k. It’s like a museum of browsers.

Ada [Website Administrator]: People still use MSN TV2 in some regions. I had one support ticket last month about that. So yes, compatibility is real.

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: Sorry—MSN TV2 is a device, not a browser, right? I just need to understand from a payments standpoint.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: It’s more like a set-top browser. We must ensure basic pages render and the credit card form works. From your end, it’s just HTTPS calls to your API; from ours, old DOM quirks.

Amina [Business Owner]: Payment is crucial. We only process credit cards through CyberSource. No other gateways. And for people uncomfortable with cards, we’ll have prepaid cards—$5, $10, $20—buyable by card or mail-in.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: And they can redeem those via a PIN to pay for a stream. We should let them check whether a card has balance by entering the PIN, quickly.

Ada [Website Administrator]: I’d love a tiny box on the site where a customer types the PIN and it returns the status instantly—like in a few seconds. Also, only admins should be able to activate those cards in the back-office.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: Yeah, we’ll ensure activation is an admin-only function. We can target the admin activation to run in, say, around three seconds if the network’s healthy—definitely staying under five seconds.

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: On authorizations, our typical turnaround is fast, but it may vary. We can commit to something around, hm, fifteen seconds worst case during the payment step. Usually it’s much faster.

Amina [Business Owner]: Good. From the user side, it must feel snappy. I don’t want a page that sits there. Page to page, clicking around should be, I don’t know, under five seconds response time ideally. People drop off otherwise.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: Let’s talk flows. Registration has to be short. For pay-as-you-go I’d say under five minutes. For an unlimited subscription—longer because of terms and card verification—but not more than ten minutes.

Ada [Website Administrator]: And an absolute maximum of four pages to make an account. That’s my hill to die on. We can compress fields and do a clear step indicator.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: Four pages max is doable. One thing: we’ll store their preferences—like most used payment method, favorite categories—so repeat purchases are quicker.

Amina [Business Owner]: Yes, we should remember what they like. Novices shouldn’t get lost. Also, we need to use familiar symbols—like a cart, a search magnifying glass, ratings stars—standard web conventions. No reinventing basic UI.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: And after logging in, I don’t want them clicking five different tabs. It should take, like, two clicks to get to their purchased streams.

Ada [Website Administrator]: So, after login, a “My Movies” button right on the dashboard and then the stream link. Two clicks. Perfect.

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: Quick question: you mentioned mail-in payments for prepaid. We’re not involved in that, but you’ll need a way to flag those orders manual-processed to generate PINs. And a batch export—Excel, right?

Ada [Website Administrator]: Yes, please. I want to generate PINs in bulk and export to Excel with the unique codes. It shouldn’t take long—like a minute or two to generate a batch.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: That’s fine. We’ll do a unique PIN generator in admin with CSV/Excel export. Also, we’ll log all sales events. Management needs to see those quickly.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: I want to pull sales and usage reports in under two minutes from the console—no fiddling. And daily usage stats automatically logged so I can assess peaks. Oh, and the homepage should automatically refresh every Friday with the latest four movies.

Amina [Business Owner]: Right, the “New This Week” slot. And we have to keep the page bright, colorful, animated on the home page. We said we’re using Flash for the homepage animations—it’s fine.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: We’ll keep the Flash animation on the home page. We can’t put it everywhere, but for the hero area that’s okay. The rest of the site must still be accessible for old browsers.

Ada [Website Administrator]: Content—search by title, actor, director. And browse by categories we define. When you open a movie’s page, it should show the description, and the cast, director info we entered in the admin.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: Reviews. Let customers post reviews, but I want to moderate—so nothing goes live until we approve. We can’t risk something offensive on the site.

Amina [Business Owner]: Agreed. We should not offend any religions or tribes. The visual design must be respectful, inclusive. Not just Nigeria. Pan-African, but we also don’t want non-Africans to feel excluded.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: Got it. Also, the streaming pipeline: we’ll encode WMP 9.0 streams at 56k and 256k. That covers very slow and basic broadband. Max buffering at start—can we aim for ten seconds or less? Anything beyond that, user connection latency is the cause.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: Yes, under ten seconds startup buffering is acceptable. During playback, if it re-buffers beyond normal, it should be because of the user’s connection.

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: From the payment steps, the purchase screen will pre-authorize cards before we let them stream, correct?

Ada [Website Administrator]: Right. No stream until the charge is authorized. Same for prepaid: the PIN must be valid with balance. And we restrict purchases to registered users; guests can browse but can’t buy.

Amina [Business Owner]: Viewing window is critical! Two days after purchase they can watch. Then it should stop. Make that very clear on the site. We’ll verify this and other comfort metrics with an anonymous survey soon after launch.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: We’ll enforce a 48-hour window from the transaction timestamp. The system won’t deliver a stream link after it expires.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: We must keep the site up all the time. 24/7 availability. We promised 99.5% uptime in the pitch deck.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: With our existing hardware, it’s achievable if we keep things lean. We’ll need to degrade gracefully: if the streaming server is down, browsing still works, purchase flows maybe disabled or queued. If the payment gateway is down, people can still browse and we display a polite message; we’ll temporarily disable purchases until it’s back.

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: On gateway downtime, we can send specific error codes. You’ll want to catch those and not show a scary error. Just say “temporarily unavailable.”

Ada [Website Administrator]: And we need the help/FAQ section plus the 24-hour toll-free support number on the site. People will definitely call at 3am.

Amina [Business Owner]: Yeah, customer support is 24/7. And internally, Velkat supports us 7am to 7pm weekdays, right?

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: That’s our admin support window. We’ll publish that to your admin team so they know when to call us directly.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: Scalability. How many simultaneous users can the web server handle? And how many concurrent streams on the streaming server?

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: On the current streaming box, we’re targeting about 50 simultaneous streams reliably. Max catalog per streaming server: around 700 movies. If we need more, we add another streaming server. For the main web app, aiming at 1,000 concurrent users. Overall we should be able to support 100,000 registered customers in the database.

Amina [Business Owner]: That’s fine as long as we can add servers later. Budget wise, we need this to run within our maintenance budget for at least two years without surprises.

Ada [Website Administrator]: Admin tasks—you said add or remove movies quickly. I need to be able to do that in under, say, five minutes total, including metadata. Categories, too, add/remove within five minutes. But ideally updating category labels in under two minutes.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: New products—like if we define a new item, I want the workflow to be quick. Two minutes to add a new product configuration, like a new prepaid batch.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: We’ll streamline the forms and pre-fill standards. Also, promo updates: if you want to swap banners or text on the marketing pages, we’ll make it so it doesn’t take more than a day to roll out a change. Not instant, but not dragging on.

Amina [Business Owner]: Good. On privacy, the policy link must be visible on all pages, and we have to tell customers when we update the policy. And we only disclose private info per that policy. No random data sharing.

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: Please also ensure card data is encrypted at rest. And restrict access to that and to sales data—only authorized administrators.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: We’ll encrypt card data in the database. Actually, wait—correction: we don’t store full PAN unless required. But anything we do store—tokens, last four—will be encrypted. Customer records and sales info will be behind admin auth with proper roles.

Ada [Website Administrator]: And weekly data integrity checks, please. A script that verifies referential integrity, looks for anomalies. If something’s off, I want an alert.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: Also, any abuse—like someone trying to brute-force PINs—should trigger an alert to administrators. We can block them or throttle.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: Yes, we’ll add abuse protection, throttling for PIN lookups, and email alerts. And validation so bad inputs don’t corrupt our data.

Amina [Business Owner]: We need antivirus on the streaming server. I know it’s not typical for video servers, but we’ve had scares before.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: We can install anti-virus. It adds overhead, but it’s manageable.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: Let’s not forget licensing. We have to meet the licensing requirements to stream these films. The system needs to support the controls we promised the rights holders—like the two-day window and proper reporting.

Ada [Website Administrator]: Speaking of which, I want the sales and usage logs accessible so I can send reports. All sales recorded, obviously.

Amina [Business Owner]: Another thing: the owner account—me—should have free access to all movies. Complimentary privileges. I need to do demos anytime.

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: Does that mean zero-amount authorization or bypass of charge? You’ll want to ensure the system recognizes a privileged account without attempting a payment.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: We’ll flag the owner role to bypass payment checks. It still logs the viewing, no charge.

Ada [Website Administrator]: On accounts: customers should be able to cancel themselves. It should be quick—like two minutes tops. When they cancel, mark them inactive so they can’t log in. But we keep their records for reporting and licensing; do not purge—keep the registered customer information in the database even after cancellation.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: And I want the power to deactivate a customer account from admin in under a minute. Sometimes we have to block access immediately.

Amina [Business Owner]: Make sure login is fast—under five seconds. Once they enter email and password, they should land right in their dashboard.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: That’s feasible. We’re cautious about heavy queries on login. Caching where possible.

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: Authorization and capture step: your checkout page should be crisp. But please set the expectation in the UI that card processing can take up to about 15 seconds during peak. We’ll aim for less.

Ada [Website Administrator]: We’ll show a spinner with polite text. Also, we need a Help/FAQ page explaining prepaid—how to buy online with card or mail-in, and how to receive the code by email or print it. People get confused.

Amina [Business Owner]: The tone has to be friendly and simple. After two weeks, we want the majority of users—like seventy percent—comfortable purchasing. After a month, nearly everyone—like ninety-five percent—should be fine with prepaid cards. Let’s run an anonymous pulse survey to measure both.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: Also test novices. The whole buy-and-play flow should be under three minutes for a single movie, once they’re registered.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: That means minimal steps: choose movie, pay, play. And streaming begins within ten seconds of buffering, ideally.

Ada [Website Administrator]: The catalog: I’ll maintain categories, keep them fresh. Also, can we make promotions update within a day? Sometimes legal wants new disclaimers.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: Yes, within a day for promos. And just to confirm—this is a web-only service. No desktop app. Everything in the browser over the Internet.

Amina [Business Owner]: Correct. And code-wise, stay compliant—W3C standards, and for PHP follow PEAR coding conventions. It’s part of our vendor agreement.

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: No issues from our side. Our integration guide works with those stacks.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: Search function: not just titles—actors and directors too. That’s essential. And the movie detail page should show the description, cast, director as we entered them.

Ada [Website Administrator]: And reviews: customers can add one for a film, others can read, but nothing goes live until I approve in admin. That’s standard.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: We’ll build the workflow: submitted state, approve/reject, publish. Timers on the admin screen so you can do it quickly.

Amina [Business Owner]: Visual branding—bright color palette. It should feel fun but respectful. Avoid anything that could be misread across tribes or religions. Include pan-African imagery without stereotyping.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: Maybe that’s a bit subjective. Let’s do user tests across Yoruba, Igbo, Hausa groups, and also non-African viewers. But also, watch the Flash—some people dislike it.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: We’re constrained to use Flash for home animations. We’ll keep it light so it doesn’t get in the way for legacy setups.

Ada [Website Administrator]: On security: admins only for sensitive things: customer records, sales reports, prepaid activation. And we limit access by role. Also hide card data—never show full numbers.

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: We prefer tokens. As for the database, you have to encrypt any stored sensitive elements. And ensure that only the authorized team can view sales data. We can audit if needed.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: We’ll put in role-based access. Also, weekly scripts to check database integrity. If anything looks corrupt, we’ll flag and roll back. And we’ll implement throttling to protect from intentional abuse—like too many failed logins or PIN tries—and notify administrators each time those thresholds are tripped.

Amina [Business Owner]: Availability: we can’t go dark. If the stream server goes down, don’t crash the entire site. People should still browse, read info, contact support. Same with payment gateway outages.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: Capacity: remind me—we can onboard up to 100,000 customers. For concurrent, 1,000 users active on the site, 50 streaming simultaneously per streaming server, and we cap at 700 movies per streaming node. If the catalog grows, we add nodes.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: Exactly. And we’ll put an antivirus on streaming servers. It’s a bit unusual, but we’ll schedule scans during low-traffic hours to limit impact.

Ada [Website Administrator]: Users need to update their billing and contact info in My Account. And when they cancel, as we said, make them inactive—no login—yet keep their profile for records in the DB.

Amina [Business Owner]: And we’ll need to deploy this quickly. Our board is pushing for a rollout that installs within a single working day. Is that still realistic?

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: For the environment we discussed, yes, if we’re reusing existing hardware, which we are. We’ll prep scripts to deploy in hours.

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: Don’t forget about audit logs—keep a trail of sales, usage. Admins need to access those. It also helps with licensing reports.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: Which reminds me: we promised weekly homepage updates showing the four newest movies. That must be automatic. No one should manually curate every Friday.

Ada [Website Administrator]: We’ll set a scheduled job that grabs the latest four by publish date, flips it every Friday at, say, 6am.

Amina [Business Owner]: For the Help content, list the toll-free 800 number, 24/7, and cover the usual friction points: how to use the prepaid PIN, how long the viewing window is, what to do if CyberSource is down, basic browser support.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: Understood. Also, we should set expectations in the UI: “Login usually takes a second or two,” “Payments may take up to 15 seconds,” and for streaming “Your video will start shortly—usually under 10 seconds.”

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: And if a policy changes—say, how we store data—you’ll email or notify in the app to users who have accounts, right?

Ada [Website Administrator]: Yes, we’ll implement a banner or an email blast for policy changes. The privacy policy link will be in the footer, visible on all pages, before we collect any form data.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: I want us to run a pulse survey a couple of weeks after launch to see comfort with purchasing. If we don’t hit seventy percent of users feeling comfortable, we revisit the flow. For prepaid, check again after a month—aim at ninety-five percent. Make both surveys anonymous so people answer honestly.

Amina [Business Owner]: And we need standard internet UI conventions. No weird scroll hijacks. Big readable fonts, clear CTAs. Keep it clean and concise.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: Noted. Also, remind me—we’re logging all streaming sales, yes. And daily usage stats, both accessible by admins. We’ll put that under “Reports.”

Ada [Website Administrator]: People call me when they see failures, so let’s make sure our outage messages are clear and non-alarming. If checkout is down, tell them to try again later; no background charges.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: We can do that. If the gateway is down, we put the site into a “browse-only” mode and show a non-blocking alert.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: About the cancel flow: customers shouldn’t have to call. It should take them under two minutes. A big “Cancel Subscription” button and a confirmation. Then the account turns inactive immediately.

Amina [Business Owner]: Great. And yes, keep all these flows short because novices will abandon. Two clicks to get to a movie after login. And if they want to use prepaid, they should be able to quickly see balance after entering their PIN.

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: Quick last note: refrain from storing full card data. Encryption is mandatory for whatever you keep, but ideally just store tokens and last four. Access restricted to authorized Izogn admins only.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: That’s our plan. Role-based permissions for customer records and sales reports. We’ll log access attempts too.

Amina [Business Owner]: Okay, final point—license compliance. We’re going to document our adherence to the rights holders’ requirements in our admin workflows—reporting, viewing window, no downloads, all that. And we’re web-only. No apps, no extra devices beyond what we already said.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: All right, we’ve covered a lot. Next steps: mock up the sign-up flow—four screens. Show me how the “owner free access” looks in the UI. And the Friday auto-refresh slot for “Latest 4.”

Ada [Website Administrator]: I’ll prepare admin screenshots for adding/removing movies and categories—aiming for under five minutes end to end. Also the quick category relabel in under two minutes, and adding a prepaid product in two minutes.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: I’ll spec the performance thresholds: 5-second max for UI responses, 15 seconds for credit card, buffering under 10 seconds. And implement abuse monitoring with admin email alerts.

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: I’ll send our API docs, and we can run a test to confirm the fallback behavior when our gateway is unavailable—your site keeps browsing live and checkout is disabled with a clear message.

Amina [Business Owner]: Excellent. Let’s keep the look bright and inviting—fun, but respectful. I don’t want any imagery that alienates non-Africans or disrespects any group. Thank you all.

Kofi [Izogn Manager]: Done. Talk soon.

Ada [Website Administrator]: Thanks everyone.

Chinedu [Velkat Tech Support]: Cheers.

Priya [CyberSource Partner]: Thanks, bye for now.

source_texts.title: Admin Console & Operations Deep-Dive — raw notes
source_texts.text:
Date: 2025-02-12
Room/Link: Lagos HQ Conf Rm B + Teams link (audio dropped twice)
Attendees:
- Amina (Owner/Founder)
- Kofi (Izogn Manager - product/ops)
- Ada (Website Admin)
- Chinedu (Velkat Tech Support)
- Blessing (Customer Support lead)
- Priya (CyberSource partner)

Notes (messy):
- Scope today = admin console tasks, ops reliability, reporting, compliance (licensing/privacy). Also support flows.
- Keep within current hardware. Web-only access. No native apps. [reconfirmed]
- Browsers: IE 5.x, IE 6, Netscape 6/7/8, Firefox 1.0; also MSN TV2. [Chinedu groaned]
- Streaming: WMP 9.0; encode two bitrates 56k, 256k. Chinedu: “We’ll test both.”
- “Home page animation stays in Flash” — Amina. Keep it light so legacy setups aren’t bogged down.

Admin tasks speed targets:
- Add/remove a movie: Ada says goal < 5 min incl. metadata entry + poster upload. DB save to make it visible immediately.
- Add/remove categories: want < 5 min. Also quick relabeling of categories in < 2 min (duplicate? but keep both metrics) [contradiction?].
- Add new “product” (e.g., prepaid batch): < 2 min. [Kofi insists]
- Generate unique prepaid PINs, bulk: within ~2 min for a batch; must export to Excel (.xls ok, .csv also ok?). [Priya suggested csv; Ada prefers xls]
- Activate a prepaid card: admin-only; action should take ~3–5 sec, target < 5 sec.
- Deactivate a customer: under 1 min; access revoked immediately.
- Weekly home page refresh: auto every Friday, show newest 4 movies (by publish date). If fewer than 4, fill with most recent.

Customer-facing flows (but admin affects):
- Registration flows: pay-as-you-go under 5 min; unlimited sub under 10 min (longer T&Cs). No more than 4 pages to create account. [Kofi wants progress bar]
- Login target: < 5 sec to authenticate and land on dashboard.
- Access to purchased movie: 2 clicks from dashboard after login (“My Movies” then “Play”).
- Purchase-to-play: under 3 min for a single movie (novice benchmark). Blessing: “We should track this time.”
- Prepaid cards: denominations $5/$10/$20; purchase with credit card OR mail-in option; distribution via email OR printable paper card. [Mail-in needs manual verification step]
- Prepaid PIN status check for customers (self-service): return balance/validity fast—under ~5 sec; throttle to prevent abuse.
- Only registered customers can purchase streaming content. Guests browse only.
- Viewing window: 2 days post-purchase. After that, no streaming allowed. Clear messaging.
- Free trial: manager-configurable parameters (duration, content in trial? TBD). Kofi to define allowed params.
- Owner comp access: Owner acct can stream all titles without payment (still log usage).
- Usability baseline: aim for ~90% of novice users to complete basic tasks without assistance; verify with post-launch surveys.

Payments & CyberSource:
- Must use CyberSource API for credit card processing; no second gateway.
- Auth must occur before streaming is allowed. [strict]
- CC processing time: keep UX; show spinner if longer than ~2 sec; worst case ~15 sec. Priya says 15 is normal upper bound.
- If CyberSource is down: site still works for browsing & help; show non-blocking alert on checkout; disable purchases until service returns; users can try again later (no queuing of charges).

Reliability & performance:
- General UI response target: < 5 sec per user action (pages, buttons). Exception: CC processing (<=15 sec), streaming buffer start (<=10 sec + user latency).
- Streaming available on-demand any time. 24/7 site ops. Uptime target 99.5%.
- If streaming server down: continue normal site ops (browsing, account, help). Hide/disable Play with explanatory message.
- Capacity figures: Streaming server ~50 simultaneous connections; per streaming server catalog capacity ~700 titles (add more servers as we grow). Web server ~1,000 simultaneous users. Database ~100,000 customers supported.
- Maintain within budget for at least 2 yrs; minimize maintenance overhead; scripts for weekly checks.

Security, privacy, integrity:
- Customer records stored in secure DB; restricted access (authorized personnel only). Sales info also restricted.
- Card data: do not store full PAN; anything stored is encrypted. Priya: “Encrypt reversible data; mask displays.”
- Privacy Policy link must be visible on all pages; inform users before collecting info (e.g., forms). Notify customers of changes to info policy (banner + email). Only disclose per policy.
- Prevent data corruption: strict validation, typed fields, server-side checks.
- Abuse protection: rate-limit PIN lookups + login attempts; notify admins on suspicious activity (all occurrences?). Ada: “Yes, I want emails when thresholds hit.”
- Weekly data integrity scripts: check referential integrity, orphaned records, time window enforcement, sales log completeness.
- Anti-virus on streaming servers. Schedule scans during off-peak. [Chinedu to pick vendor]

Catalog & discovery:
- Search: by title, actor, director (exact + partial?). [lean toward partial substring]
- Categories: browsing by admin-created categories. Need quick category maintenance (see above time targets).
- Movie detail page: shows description, actor(s), director (data from admin entries). Include runtime? [maybe later]
- Reviews: customers can post reviews; admin approval required before publish; others can read reviews once approved. Moderation queue in admin.

Help & support:
- Help/FAQ section with common issues (prepaid usage, viewing window, buffering tips, legacy browsers, MSN TV2 specifics). Include 24/7 toll-free 800 number prominently.
- Velkat tech phone support to Izogn admins 7am–7pm M–F. After-hours by email only [Ada to confirm].
- Post-launch anonymous pulse surveys: at ~2 weeks target >=70% report being comfortable purchasing; at ~1 month target >=95% report being comfortable using prepaid cards.

Brand & inclusivity:
- Visual style: vibrant, fun, bright color palette, modern-clean-concise layout. African feel w/o alienating non-Africans. Respect religious/ethnic sensitivities. “Cater to all tribes in Nigeria and broader African audience.”
- Use standard web conventions, plain language, recognizable icons. Accessibility? [Not in scope today].

Reporting & admin access:
- Sales logging: all streaming transactions logged in DB; accessible to admins.
- Daily usage stats: automatically logged; admins can open reports quickly (target: under 2 min to load a report).
- Exports: Excel for prepaid PIN batches; CSV for sales and usage? [Ada: both would be nice]

Operations & deployment:
- Install time: must be installable within one working day in the specified environment. Pre-script deployments.
- Existing hardware only (constraint). No cloud shift (for now).
- Standards compliance: W3C standards; PHP code follows PEAR conventions. [code review checklists]
- Licensing: system must align w/ streaming licensing requirements; includes 2-day viewing enforcement, logging for report to rights holders.

Edge Cases / misc:
- If user network is slow: buffering may exceed 10 sec due to latency; we should message that. Otherwise we consider <10 sec normal.
- If card authorization fails: do not allow streaming; offer retry or switch to prepaid.
- Account cancellation: customer-initiated; flow < 2 min; mark inactive; block login. Keep all registered customer information in the database after cancel for records/licensing (do not purge). Admin deactivation also supported (< 1 min), immediate effect.
- Owner free access role: bypass payments. Still log usage.
- Preferences retention: store user buying preferences (payment choice, maybe favorite categories). [Privacy policy must mention]
- Promo/content updates: marketing text/artwork changes should be publishable within a day.

Action Items:
- [Ada] Draft admin screens: add/remove movie (<5 min), category maintenance (<5 min, relabel <2), customer deactivate (<1), review approval list.
- [Chinedu] Implement PIN generator + Excel export (batch <2 min). Add activation button (admin-only, <5 sec). Abuse alerts (email on suspicious activity). Weekly DB integrity script.
- [Priya] Send latest CyberSource API doc; note timeouts; sample error codes to trigger fallback messaging.
- [Kofi] Define free trial parameters (length, titles included, renew rules) [TBD document]. Confirm gateway outage messaging (disable purchase; allow retry later; no queuing).
- [Blessing] Draft Help/FAQ including 24/7 toll-free number, buffering expectations, prepaid guidance, browser support notes, MSN TV2 instructions. Include note about the anonymous surveys.
- [Chinedu] Add AV to streaming servers. Verify capacity numbers (50 streams, 700 titles per server) — test plan.
- [Ada] Implement policy-change notification (banner + email). Add privacy link to all pages. Confirm Velkat after-hours support channel.
- [Team] Ensure search covers title/actor/director with partial matches; confirm W3C and PEAR compliance checklist.

Open Issues / Parking Lot:
- Export formats: Excel only vs CSV too; performance for very large PIN batches [scale tests].
- Owner access UI — show price crossed-out? or just “Play” button? [Amina to decide]
- Define survey methodology for novice comfort targets (2-week 70% purchase comfort, 1-month 95% prepaid comfort) — anonymous; who collects, where displayed? [TBD]
- Data retention post-cancel — indefinite? [Legal check].

Scribbles/metrics (from whiteboard):
- UI resp <5s
- CC <=15s
- Buffer <=10s (+latency)
- Reg pages: max 4
- P2P time <3m
- Streams: 50
- Catalog/server: 700
- Users concurrent: 1,000
- Customers total: 100k
- Uptime 99.5%
- Install 1 day
- Novice usability ~90%

Reminders:
- Only registered users can purchase.
- No streaming after 2-day window; enforce.
- Use standard web symbols, plain words; avoid confusing jargon in UI.
- Keep brand inclusive to all tribes and religions; avoid alienating non-Africans.
- Continue site ops if streaming server down; likewise if payment gateway down (browsing unaffected; checkout disabled).
- Help/FAQ + 24/7 toll-free number must be prominent.

End of notes (audio cut off at 58:22, some items [?]).

source_texts.title: RE: Checkout timing, prepaid flow, and weekly ops - AfriStream On-Demand
source_texts.text:
Email 1
From: Kofi <kofi@izogn.com>
To: Priya <priya@cybersource.com>
CC: Amina <amina@izogn.com>, Chinedu <chinedu@velkat.io>, Ada <ada@izogn.com>, Blessing <support@izogn.com>
Subject: Checkout timing + gateway fallback (AfriStream)
Date: Tue, 2025-03-04 09:12:27 +0100

Hi Priya,

We’re tightening our checkout expectations for launch. Two asks:
- End-to-end credit card authorization: we are setting user expectation at around 10s typical, but we need to cap at 15s worst-case. Can the API timeouts be tuned so our UI spinner and messaging don’t hang beyond that?
- If your gateway is unavailable, we want to keep the site operational. We’ll let users browse and we’ll disable purchasing with a clear “temporarily unavailable” message so they can try again later. Do you have standard error codes we can catch for a clear message vs generic failure?

Also confirming: we process all cards via CyberSource only (no other gateways), and we’ll not store full PANs—just tokens/last4. Anything stored is encrypted and accessible to our admins only.

Thanks,
Kofi

---

Email 2
From: Priya <priya@cybersource.com>
To: Kofi <kofi@izogn.com>
CC: Amina <amina@izogn.com>, Chinedu <chinedu@velkat.io>, Ada <ada@izogn.com>, Blessing <support@izogn.com>
Subject: RE: Checkout timing + gateway fallback (AfriStream)
Date: Tue, 2025-03-04 12:01:10 +0100

Hi Kofi,

15 seconds as an upper-bound aligns with our typical SLA for auth; spikes are rare but possible. We’ll share sample code that sets your client timeout and retries gracefully.

For outages, we emit specific codes (e.g., network vs issuer vs system). You can display a “temporarily unavailable” message and disable the final purchase button without crashing the session. Let users retry when service returns; do not queue a charge server-side without a new confirmation.

Please also ensure your privacy policy is linked upfront at checkout, so you’re communicating info practices before capturing details. If the policy changes, notify users (banner/email). Standard compliance.

Regards,
Priya

---

Email 3
From: Ada <ada@izogn.com>
To: Team <all@izogn.com>
CC: Priya <priya@cybersource.com>
Subject: Re: Prepaid + Admin Console time targets (draft)
Date: Wed, 2025-03-05 08:45:03 +0100

Hi all,

Here’s what I’m planning for prepaid and admin flows (feedback welcome):

1) Admin-only prepaid activation: Button in Admin > Prepaid. Target action time under 5 seconds. 
2) Generate PIN batches: Unique codes, export to Excel. Batch generation in under 2 minutes. Values: $5/$10/$20 only.
3) Customer view: “Check your PIN status” box on prepaid page. Enter code, see balance/validity in ~5 sec. Rate limit to avoid abuse. 
4) Distribution: If purchased online, we email the PIN. Also provide a printable version (PDF). For mail-in payments, we’ll mark as “verified” manually and then generate/send.
5) Only registered users can purchase content. Guests can browse/search only. 
6) Free trial: I’ll add a manager-config screen. Parameters TBD by Kofi (length, allowed titles). 
7) Reporting: Sales logs visible to admins; daily usage stats logs under Reports. I want the “open report” action to load in under 2 minutes.
8) Account management: My Account page for updating billing/contact info. Cancellation flow to be simple; under 2 minutes and marks account inactive (blocking login). Admins can deactivate users in under a minute. We keep customer records in the DB for reporting/licensing after cancel—no deletion.

We’ll also keep the Friday auto-update of the “Latest 4” on the homepage. And yes, Flash animation remains on home.

Ada

---

Email 4
From: Chinedu <chinedu@velkat.io>
To: Ada <ada@izogn.com>
CC: Kofi <kofi@izogn.com>, Amina <amina@izogn.com>, Priya <priya@cybersource.com>, Blessing <support@izogn.com>
Subject: RE: Prepaid + Admin Console time targets (draft)
Date: Wed, 2025-03-05 10:22:51 +0100

Ada,

All sounds good. Adding technical confirmations and constraints:

- We’ll keep general UI responses under ~5 seconds, except: credit card up to ~15 sec, streaming start buffer <=10 sec (beyond that it’s on the user’s network). 
- Streaming uses WMP 9.0. We’ll encode 56k and 256k bitrates as planned. On-demand 24/7 (site target uptime 99.5%). 
- If streaming server is down: site doesn’t die; we’ll hide/disable “Play” and show a message. If payment gateway is down: browsing unaffected; checkout disabled with clear messaging.
- Capacity: streaming server 50 concurrent streams; 700 titles per streaming server. Web tier 1,000 simultaneous users. DB ok for 100k customers. Add servers to scale the catalog when needed.
- Security: Customer records + sales data restricted to authorized admins only. Card data encrypted at rest (but we prefer tokens). Abuse protection for PIN and login attempts, with admin notifications.
- Weekly DB integrity scripts to check for corruption/anomalies. Anti-virus on streaming servers (scans off-peak). 
- We’ll deploy within one working day (scripted). Existing hardware only (reminder). Code will follow W3C and PEAR standards.
- Search: title, actor, director. Movie detail pulls description + cast + director from DB. 
- Reviews: Customers can add, but require admin approval before visible; others can read once approved.
- Owner account: flagged to bypass payments and stream all titles; usage still logged.

Minor: Please confirm the signup page count—max 4 screens—and the UX copy says clearly that the viewing window is 2 days after purchase. 

Cheers,
C.

---

Email 5
From: Blessing <support@izogn.com>
To: Team <all@izogn.com>
CC: Amina <amina@izogn.com>, Kofi <kofi@izogn.com>, Ada <ada@izogn.com>, Chinedu <chinedu@velkat.io>, Priya <priya@cybersource.com>
Subject: Re: Help content + phone support details
Date: Wed, 2025-03-05 14:18:09 +0100

Hi folks,

Drafting the Help/FAQ now. I will include:
- 24/7 toll-free 800 number on every page footer (above the fold if possible on Help page). 
- How prepaid works: buying with credit card or mail-in, receiving via email or printing the card, checking PIN status. 
- How to find a movie: search by title/actor/director, browse by categories. 
- Viewing window explanation (2 days), and that only registered users can buy/stream. 
- Basic troubleshooting for buffering (start under ~10 sec normally), and legacy browser notes (IE 5–6, Netscape 6–8, Firefox 1.0, MSN TV2 tips).
- Privacy policy: where to find it (visible on all pages), and that we’ll notify users when it changes.

Also, for novices we’ll keep language super simple and standard web conventions. I’ll add an anonymous short survey at 2 weeks post-launch to gauge comfort buying online; target ≥70% comfortable. Another anonymous one at 1 month focusing on prepaid; target ≥95% comfortable.

Blessing

---

Email 6
From: Amina <amina@izogn.com>
To: Team <all@izogn.com>
CC: Priya <priya@cybersource.com>
Subject: RE: Branding and inclusivity (quick note)
Date: Thu, 2025-03-06 08:12:44 +0100

Morning all,

Just a reminder: the visual direction must be bright, fun, and modern—but let’s avoid anything that could be offensive to any religious or ethnic group. The site should feel inclusive to all Africans (not only Nigerians) and also friendly to non-African visitors. Use plain language and recognizable icons so novices aren’t confused.

Also, let’s ensure we retain user preferences (what they like to watch, how they prefer to pay) to smooth future purchases. And please keep the promo updates lightweight. If we need to change headline text or artwork, we should be able to push it within a day.

On usability, our goal is that roughly 90% of novice internet users can complete core tasks without assistance; we’ll validate with the anonymous surveys Blessing mentioned.

Thank you,
Amina

---

Email 7
From: Kofi <kofi@izogn.com>
To: Ada <ada@izogn.com>, Chinedu <chinedu@velkat.io>
CC: Amina <amina@izogn.com>, Priya <priya@cybersource.com>, Blessing <support@izogn.com>
Subject: RE: Loose ends before pilot — signup, cancel, reporting
Date: Thu, 2025-03-06 16:37:33 +0100

Hey,

Loose ends list (sorry for the brain dump):

- Signup: confirm max 4 pages. Pay-as-you-go time under 5 min; unlimited sub under 10 min. Login in under 5 sec.
- After login, users should hit their movies in under 2 clicks. Purchase-to-play under 3 min (we’ll measure). 
- Sales logging: every streaming sale must hit the DB. Daily usage stats automatically captured. Reports accessible by admins (target: load a report in under 2 min). 
- Data: prevent bad inputs from corrupting DB; weekly integrity scripts. Please set up admin email alerts for suspicious behavior.
- Accounts: customer self-cancel in under 2 min; mark inactive, block login. Admin deactivation under 1 min. Keep all registered customer information in the database after cancel (for reporting/licensing) — do not purge. 
- Licensing: ensure all constraints are met (2-day viewing, no stream without purchase/authorization). 
- Platform: web-only; reusing existing hardware; standards (W3C, PEAR) compliance.
- Streaming server: antivirus installed; capacity 50 streams; add servers if we exceed 700 titles on a node. 
- Homepage: autopopulate newest 4 every Friday early AM; if less than 4 new, fill with most recent. 

Open: gateway outage UX — disable purchases and show a clear message; users can retry later when the gateway is back (no background queuing).

Kofi

---

Email 8
From: Chinedu <chinedu@velkat.io>
To: Kofi <kofi@izogn.com>
CC: Ada <ada@izogn.com>, Amina <amina@izogn.com>, Priya <priya@cybersource.com>, Blessing <support@izogn.com>
Subject: RE: Loose ends before pilot — signup, cancel, reporting
Date: Fri, 2025-03-07 09:02:21 +0100

Kofi,

We’re aligned on most items. Answers and notes:

- Signup/login timings: achievable. We’ll instrument timers to confirm real-world numbers.
- “My Movies” direct path = 2 clicks after login — confirmed.
- Sales/usage logs: admin can access; we’ll add role-based restrictions so only authorized personnel can see sales/customer records.
- Data integrity + abuse: validation server-side; weekly scripts; email notifications on anomalies and aggressive PIN or login retries.
- Cancellation/deactivation: both flows implemented as described. Records retained post-cancel in the DB.
- Licensing & enforcement: 2-day window enforced; streaming only after CyberSource auth or valid prepaid PIN; no guest purchases.
- Infra/standards: we remain web-only on existing hardware; W3C + PEAR compliance checklist done.
- Streaming servers: AV will be installed; we’ll schedule nightly scans. Capacity targets in place.
- Homepage: Friday 6am refresh to show latest 4. 

Pending: Priya will confirm the exact codes we should trap for gateway outage. We’ll keep checkout disabled during outages and let users try again later.

Side note: We’ll keep the privacy policy in the footer across all pages and add an in-app notification when it changes. We’ll also display the 24/7 toll-free number prominently in help.

— C.

(sent from mobile, forgive typos)

candidate_pool.text: Website shall allow customers to purchase pre-paid cards of $5 $10 or $20 value either by credit card or mail-in payment option.
The website will comply with W3C standards.
Users should only have to navigate through a maximum of 4 pages to create an account with the website.
The system will provide a 24 hour 800 toll free number for support to its customers.
System shall let administrator de-activate a customer account in under 1 minute. Customer will no longer be able to access the website.
Website must interface with the CyberSource API to process credit card transactions.
The website will provide a help section with answers and solutions to common problems.
The system shall enforce per-title geographic restrictions by blocking playback for IP addresses not included in the title’s allowed country list at the time of playback request.
Movies shall be encoded and streamed via Windows Media Player 9.0.
Only authorized personnel can access customer records in the database.
The system shall send an email reminder to the purchaser 36 hours after a rental purchase to inform them that 12 hours remain in the 2-day viewing window.
System shall let administrator add/remove movies on the website in under 5 minutes. Entered movie information will be stored in the database and will now be available on the website.
A customer can only stream a movie if they purchased it and are within the 2 day viewing period.
System shall let customers register on the website as a unlimited movie subscriber user in under 10 minutes.
The system shall provide an Order History page where customers can view all past purchases with purchase date, amount paid, and remaining viewing time for any active rentals.
Administrator shall be able to activate a pre-paid card via the Administration section in under 5 seconds.
The system shall allow registered customers to add movies to a personal Watchlist and access that Watchlist from the dashboard in a single click.
Website must be fully operational with MSN TV2.
Website shall allow customers to add their own movie review for a selected movie.
Promotional updates to the website should take a day to update.
The website shall be available for use 24 hours per day 365 days per year.

Website shall allow customers to view reviews of selected movies by other customers.

The streaming server will support 50 simultaneous streaming connections.
An anonymous survey will show that 95% of customers are comfortable using and purchasing pre-paid cards after a month of using the system.

seed_registry.requirement_text: The system shall persist a viewer’s playback position for each purchased movie and resume playback from the last position within the 2-day viewing window on subsequent plays.

brief.domain: OTT video streaming and digital storefront
business_goals: Monetize African films globally via secure online sales and rentals
Offer flexible payment options including credit cards and pre-paid cards
Deliver a friendly experience that builds trust with novice internet users
Maintain 24/7 reliable service and scale to a large customer base
Ensure compliance with streaming licenses, web standards, and privacy practices
Provide administrators with clear sales and usage insights for decision-making
core_features: Search and browse the movie catalog by title, actor, director, and category
On-demand streaming in Windows Media Player with a limited 2-day viewing window after purchase
Registration and login for pay-as-you-go or unlimited subscription plans, including manager-configured free trials
Payments: credit-card authorization plus buy/redeem pre-paid cards ($5/$10/$20) with email or printable delivery and a mail-in option
Customer reviews on movies with administrator approval before publishing
Account and assistance: update billing/contact info, cancel accounts, on-site Help/FAQ, 24/7 toll-free support; owner account has complimentary access
Admin console for catalog/category maintenance, customer de/activation, unique PIN generation with Excel export, weekly newest-4 homepage refresh, and sales/usage reporting
quality_concerns: Performance: pages respond within a few seconds, purchase authorization completes quickly, and buffering stays under about 10 seconds
Usability for novices: short, clear flows (account setup in few steps/minutes; access a movie in very few clicks; purchase-to-play kept brief)
Availability and resilience: always-on service with high uptime and graceful operation during streaming or payment outages
Security, privacy, and retention: encrypt card data, restrict access to customer/sales records, publish/privacy updates, remember user preferences
Data integrity and abuse protection: strong validation, weekly integrity checks, admin alerts on suspicious activity, anti-virus on streaming servers
Scalability and capacity: support many simultaneous users/streams and a sizable catalog, with ability to add servers as needed
Inclusive UX and brand: modern, vibrant African aesthetic that is welcoming to non-Africans and respectful of all tribes and religions
constraints: Web-only access using the existing hosting hardware
Support legacy browsers (IE 5–6, Netscape 6–8, Firefox 1.0) and MSN TV2
Process credit cards via the CyberSource API
Encode and stream using Windows Media Player 9 at 56k and 256k bitrates
Home page animations implemented with Flash
Comply with W3C standards and PHP PEAR coding conventions
Obtain appropriate streaming licenses; deployment installable within one working day
